zh.Bollin Fee, Wilmslow (Le Bolyn), Cheshir: 英國英格蘭爾波利 費
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Thornton Fitton於1345年出生在英國爾時，她的父親Hammon Fitton21歲，母親Elizabeth THORNTON18歲。她和Richard Venables, Knight至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1382年在她的家鄉享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: Fitton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Middle English fiton, fittin ‘lie, falsehood’, for a lier or cheat.

possibly a habitational name from Fitton Hall in Leverington, Cambridgeshire, named in Anglo-Scandinavian as ‘settlement by the meadow-land’, from Old English tūn + Old Norse fit, a term denoting grassland on the bank of a river.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Thornton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from the very numerous places named with Old English þorn ‘thorn bush’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. The given name is associated with the American writer Thornton Wilder ( 1897–1975 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Thornton Fitton was born in 1345, in Cheshire, England, her father, Hammon Fitton, was 21 and her mother, Elizabeth THORNTON, was 18. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Richard Venables, Knight. She died in 1382, in her hometown, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Thornton Fitton est née en 1345, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Hammon Fitton, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth THORNTON, avait 18 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Richard Venables, Knight. Elle est décédée en 1382, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 37 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Thornton Fitton(이)가 1345년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hammon Fitton(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth THORNTON(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 Richard Venables, Knight(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1382년 그녀의 고향에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.
